claim: The hybrid-transitional coding may understate the sacrificial-escalation logic of the post-9/11 War on Terror: the sacred-enemy framing of jihadist Islam, the open-ended sacrifice demands of indefinite war, and the suspension of habeas corpus and torture authorization exhibit structural features of high-pathology cases rather than hybrid transition.
rationale: The post-9/11 case is hybrid only if institutional constraints ultimately contained the sacrificial logic; critics argue those constraints were progressively dismantled (Patriot Act, AUMF, Abu Ghraib, Guantanamo) such that the period is better characterized as sacrificial-escalation with incomplete institutional resistance.
